title: Attributes
text:
AttributesChangelogsOld Abilities
For a list of heroes sorted by attributes, see Table of hero attributes.
Attributes are the main statistics of all heroes that independently determine most scaling statistics. These attributes are  strength,  agility, and  intelligence. Attributes are gained when a hero levels up, obtains a  talent, buys certain items, or uses certain abilities. Illusions benefit from attributes.

Contents

2 Strength
3 Agility

8.1 Talents Granting Attribute Combinations

9 Abilities Modifying Attributes

11 Recent Changes

Primary Attribute[edit]
Every hero has a primary attribute, which modifies their main attack damage per 1 point of primary attribute. A hero's primary attribute heavily impacts and commonly even defines the hero's role and how they are played. For example,  intelligence heroes are typically more dependent on abilities. A hero's primary attribute typically grows faster than the other two attributes.

Abilities Modifying Primary Attributes
Morphling - Morph
Duration: 24 ( 36)Copies the target hero's primary attribute while active.

Strength[edit]
Main Article: Strength
 Strength is a measure of a hero's toughness and endurance. Strength determines a hero's maximum health and health regeneration. Heroes with strength as their primary attribute can be hard to kill, so they often take initiator and tank roles, initiating fights and taking most of the damage from enemy attacks.
Every point in  strength increases:

Maximum health by 22.
Health regeneration by 0.1.
For strength heroes, every point in strength also increases main attack damage by 1.

Agility[edit]
Main Article: Agility
 Agility is a measure of a hero's swiftness and dexterity. Agility determines a hero's armor and attack speed. Heroes with agility as their primary attribute tend to be more dependent on their physical attacks and items, and are usually capable of falling back on their abilities in a pinch. Agility heroes often take carry and gank roles.
Every point in  agility increases:

Base armor by 0.167.
Attack speed by 1.
For agility heroes, every point in agility also increases main attack damage by 1.

Intelligence[edit]
Main Article: Intelligence
 Intelligence is a measure of a hero's wit and wisdom. Intelligence determines a hero's maximum mana, mana regeneration and magic resistance. Heroes with intelligence as their primary attribute tend to rely on their abilities to deal damage or help others. Intelligence heroes often take support, gank, and pusher roles.
Every point in  intelligence increases:

Maximum mana by 12.
Mana regeneration by 0.05.
Magic resistance by 0.1%.
For intelligence heroes, every point in intelligence also increases main attack damage by 1.

Universal[edit]
Main Article: Universal
 Universal heroes are like jack of all trades and benefit from every attribute. While they dont gain that much from building a single main attribute, their strength lies in the flexibility to choose items based on their specific abilities and bonuses not so much on the item's attributes. So they can always switch between the support and carry roles based on their itembuild, not bound by a main attribute.
Every point of  /  /  attribute increases:

Main attack damage by 0.45.

Attribute Bonus[edit]

Attribute Bonus is a passive ability available to every hero, except for certain heroes (e.g.  Invoker), located within the  Talent leveling menu, below the talent tree. It automatically levels up if nothing else can be leveled up at that level.

3 3 Attribute Bonus         AbilityPassiveAffectsSelfGrants all  attribute bonuses per level.This ability automatically levels up at certain levels if all ability points are spent on the hero's respective abilities.Min Hero Level:  2    Attribute Bonus:  2/4/6/8/10/12/14    DetailsMisc.Show All
  The Attribute Bonus UI within Talent leveling menu.
Requires hero level 2 to be unlocked.
The min levels required per level of Attribute Bonus are level 2/4/6/8/10/12/14.
If all ability points are spend on the hero's respective abilities, this ability levels up automatically on level 15/16/17/19/20/21/22.
math

Items Granting Attributes[edit]
Each attribute has its own set of items that focus on boosting it. These items are different for each attribute. In order to categorize them, they're split into 5 different sections:

Tiny Items consist only of  Iron Branch and boosts all attributes by 1. This item is usually taken as a starting item to make up for gaps in a hero's stats.
Small Items boost their associated attribute by 3, or all attributes by 2. These items are usually taken as starting items to make up for gaps in a hero's stats.
Medium Items boost their associated attribute by 6, or all attributes by 4. Though generally not taken to increase attributes, these items are usually components of many midrange items.
Large Items boost their associated attribute by 10, or all attributes by 6. These items are usually components in more powerful items, and almost never bought just for the stats.
Ultimate Items boost their associated attribute by 25, or all attributes by 15. These are mostly used to make late game items because of how expensive they are and how cost-ineffective they are per point of stats.
Besides the items in the table,  Ghost Scepter is another basic item that boosts all attributes by 5. All other items that grant attributes are upgrades, and are all derived from one or multiple of the 17 items listed below.

Abilities Modified by Attributes[edit]
Abilities Modified by Attributes
Centaur Warrunner - Double Edge
Strength as Damage: 0.6/0.9/1.2/1.5 ( 0.9/1.2/1.5/1.8)Max Stacks:  5Strength Bonus per Stack:  15%Buff Duration:  15Double Edge's damage consists of a flat amount, plus a percentage of the caster's strength.
Centaur Warrunner - Stampede
Strength as Damage: 2/2.5/3The damage of Stampede is entirely based on the caster's strength.
Drow Ranger - Precision Aura
Agility as Ranged Agility Bonus: 0.1The agility bonus is entirely based on the agility of the hero with this ability.
Ethereal Blade - Ether Blast
Primary Attribute as Damage: Ether Blast's damage consists of a flat amount, plus a percentage of the target's primary attribute.
Huskar - Berserker's Blood
Strength as Max Health Regen Bonus: 0.25/0.4/0.55/0.7 ( 0.55/0.7/0.85/1)The health regeneration is based on the strength of the hero with this ability.
Morphling - Adaptive Strike (Agility)
Min Bonus Damage Agility Multiplier: Max Bonus Damage Agility Multiplier: The damage is based on the caster's agility to strength ratio, plus a fixed amount. The minimum multiplier is used when the caster's strength is 50% higher than their agility, and the maximum when agility is 50% higher than strength.
Morphling - Adaptive Strike (Strength)
Min Knockback Distance: Max Knockback Distance: Min Stun Duration: Max Stun Duration: The stun duration and knockback distance are based on the caster's strength to agility ratio. The minimum duration and distance are used when the caster's agility is 50% higher than their strength, and the maximum when strength is 50% higher than agility.
Ogre Magi - Multicast
Multicast Chance Increase per Strength: 0.0625%He receives mana and mana regeneration from Strength. Increases Multicast chance by 1% for each 16 Strength Ogre has.
Pudge - Dismember
Strength as Damage/Heal per Second: ( )Dismember's damage and self-heal consist of a flat amount, plus a percentage of the caster's strength.
Riki - Backstab
Hero Attack Damage Bonus per Agi: 0.55 ( 0.85)The bonus damage of Backstab is entirely based on the agility of the hero with this ability.
Silencer - Glaives of Wisdom
Intelligence as Damage: 2/3/4/5 ( )The damage of Glaives of Wisdom is entirely based on the caster's intelligence.
Silencer - Last Word
Intelligence Difference Damage Multiplier: 1/1.5/2/2.5Last Word's damage consists of a flat part, plus a based on the difference between the intelligence of the caster and the target.
Skywrath Mage - Arcane Bolt
Intelligence as Bonus Damage: 1.5 ( 3)Arcane Bolt's damage consists of a flat amount, plus a percentage of the caster's intelligence.

Recent Changes[edit]

VersionDescription

7.38(2025-02-19)

Reduced  Attribute Bonus level requirement from 6 to 2.

7.33c(2023-05-13)

Increased  Attribute Bonus level requirement from 2 to 6.

7.29(2021-04-09)

ADDED   Attribute Bonus to every hero except  Invoker .
The level-up button is located below the  talent tree.
Permanently grants the hero 2 of  all attributes when leveled up.
Can be leveled up a total of 7 times. Has no level requirements, so it can be leveled up at any level.
If no other abilities or talents can be leveled up upon reaching a new level, Attribute Bonus levels up automatically.
